rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM E. WILSON VS. SUMRAN CHOMMANARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 10, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT SUMRAN CHOMMANARD'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Granted. (CCP sec. 473.5.) Defendant has established that he did not have actual notice of the suit and his lack of actual notice was not caused by his inexcusable neglect or avoidance of service. (Declaration of Sumran Chommanard.) "It is the policy of the law to favor, wherever possible, a hearing on the merits." (Weitz v. Yankosky (1966) 63 Cal.2d 849, 854.) Defendant may file his proposed answer within 10 days of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLE NOLLETTE VS. LRICO SERVICES, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 10, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT LRICO SERVICES, LLC, DISTILLERY NO. 209 LTD. NAPA, CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication (Part 1 of 2). Motion for summary judgment is granted. As to the wrongful termination cause of action, Defendants met their burden showing that Plaintiff was terminated for a legitimate business reason - poor performance. (UMF nos. 7-14.) Plaintiff does not dispute these material facts. Plaintiff has not raised a triable issue of fact as to whether she was terminated because she inquired about her ownership rights. The fact that Plaintiff was terminated approximately one month after inquiring about her ownership rights is insufficient to establish a nexus. (See Loggins v. Kaiser Permanente Internat. (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 1102, 1113.) As to the breach of contract cause of action, Defendants met their burden by establishing that no oral contract was formed, as no specifics about Plaintiff's equity in the company were discussed. (UMF nos. 31-32.) Defendants also met their burden showing that Defendants did not breach any written agreement. (UMF no. 31-37.) Plaintiff has not raised a triable issue of fact as to whether Defendants breached a contract. Plaintiff concedes that the parties agreed that the specifics regarding equity "would be spelled out at a later date." (Plaintiff's response to UMF nos. 31-32.) The May 28, 2010 letter unambiguously provided that Plaintiff would be entitled to a "one-time cash bonus" if a liquidity event occurred while Plaintiff was working at the company. (Defendants' Appendix of Evidence, Ex. 4.) It is undisputed that a liquidity event never occurred while Plaintiff was employed by Defendants. (UMF no. 37.) As to the implied covenant cause of action, Plaintiff cannot establish that Defendants acted in such a way to deprive Plaintiff the benefits under any contract. (See Foley v. Interactive Data Corp. (1988) 47 Cal.3d 654, 683-84.) As discussed above, Plaintiff was not entitled to benefits because no liquidity event had occurred. As to the promissory estoppel cause of action, Plaintiff cannot establish that a clear and unambiguous promise was made. The evidence shows that no specifics about Plaintiff's equity in the company were discussed pre-hire. (UMF nos. 31-32.) As to the fraud cause of action, Defendants met their burden establishing any pre-hire discussions about equity were too vague to constitute a misrepresentation and, even if there was a misrepresentation, Plaintiff's reliance was not reasonable. (UMF nos. 27-30.) Plaintiff has not met her burden to establish a triable issue of fact. The evidence establishes that Plaintiff failed to object to the equity terms after receiving the May 18, 2010 letter. (UMF nos. 27-28.) Plaintiff understood the terms of the equity arrangement, as evidenced by her discussions about Kathryn Frederick's compensation package. (UMF no. 36.) Where an individual discovers the truth regarding an alleged misrepresentation and does not change her legal position, any alleged reliance is unreasonable. (Rochlis v. Walt Disney Co. (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 201, 215-16.) (Continued to Part 2.) =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JUSTIN MOORE VS. RICHARD BURDEN TEED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Donald Moore To Provide Documents Responsive To Req. For Production Of Documents And Appendix
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, July 10, 2014, Line 1 - DEFENDANT SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY, INC.'s Motion To Compel Donald Moore To Provide Documents Responsive To Request For Production Of Documents And Appendix. Pro Tem Judge Dennis Hyde, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JASON BISHOP VS. CONSUMERTRACK INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 10, 2014, Line 7, PLAINTIFFS JASON BISHOP, SHELLY CARPER, STEVEN CARPER, JULIE CHENDES, DIANA HELLMAN'S Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint. Motion granted. The emails that Plaintiffs seek to add to the Complaint were received prior to the filing of the original Complaint, and were therefore not avoidable on the grounds argued by Defendant. Moreover, as trial has not yet been set, Defendant will not suffer any prejudice in responding to these additional allegations. Furthermore, the validity of the proposed amended pleading is not generally considered by courts in deciding whether to grant leave to amend, and Defendant's proffered grounds for motion to strike are premature. (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup.Ct. (Marker, U.S.A.) (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.) Finally, Defendant's request for conditions upon granting leave is denied. Defendant failed to present any good cause to potentially deny any Plaintiffs' statutory rights. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: BERDOING &WEIL, LLP VS. 301 BRYANT STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 10, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT 301 BRYANT STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the breach of contract and money had and received causes of action are sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's remedy is to sue for quantum meruit. See Fracase v. Brent (1972) 6 Cal.3d 784. The demurrer to the quantum meruit cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff shall allege an approximate sum due. The current allegations seek a sum that "exceeds the jurisdictional maximum (sic) of this Court" and also improperly seeks to recover under the contract. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: BERDOING &WEIL, LLP VS. 301 BRYANT STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 10, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT 301 BRYANT STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The court grants the motion to strike. The court strikes the lien allegations without leave to amend. The parties' agreement is attached to the amended complaint. The agreement shows that defendant never initialed paragraph 5 and defendant did not agree to a lien. Paragraph 9 of the agreement requires a writing to amend the agreement and Plaintiff fails to allege such a writing. The court strikes the allegations regarding the fee arbitration without leave to amend. Paragraphs 13 and 14 and exhibits E and F of the amended complaint facially show that plaintiff failed to comply with the business and professions code 6201. The court strikes the damages allegations with leave to amend. Plaintiff needs to seek the reasonable value of its services. The court strikes the prayer for prejudgment interest without leave to amend. CC 3287(b) is inapplicable because it only applies to contract actions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SHARON M CASTILLO VS. TANCINCO LAW OFFICES (A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Strike Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 10, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANTS TANCINCO LAW OFFICES (A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION), LOURDES TANCINCO, JERICHO SARIA, REY TANCINCO, HADRIAN SANTOS' Motion To Strike Complaint. The anti-SLAPP motion to strike is granted in part. Defendant demonstrates the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis. Plaintiff's action implicates CCP 425.16 because the television program was an exercise of free speech regarding a public issue (immigration). Under the second prong of the analysis, plaintiff must demonstrate a probability of prevailing. Plaintiff cannot establish the constitutional right to privacy cause of action. Plaintiff cannot show a legally protected privacy interest. "A particular class of information is private when well-established social norms recognize the need to maximize individual control over its dissemination and use to prevent unjustified embarrassment or indignity. Such norms create a threshold reasonable expectation of privacy in the data at issue." Hill v. NCAA (1994) 7 Cal.4th 1, 35. In this case, plaintiff has no legitimate interest in attempting to conceal her illegal conduct/status. In addition, plaintiff cannot establish a reasonable expectation of privacy or a serious invasion. "Actionable invasions of privacy must be sufficiently serious in their nature, scope, and actual or potential impact to constitute an egregious breach of the social norms underlying the privacy right. Thus, the extent and gravity of the invasion is an indispensable consideration in assessing an alleged invasion of privacy." Id. at 37. Plaintiff cannot prevail on the publication of private facts cause of action under Shulman v. Group W Productions, Inc. (1998) 18 Cal.4th 200. The disclosure was not offensive or objectionable and the T.V. program was newsworthy. Plaintiff cannot establish the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action because plaintiff does not highlight outrageous conduct. The court denies the motion as to the negligence cause of action. Defendants stated that the program would not be re-broadcast, but it was. A factual issue remains regarding breach and causation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
